title: coelogyne coriacea
intro: Coelogyne coriacea, also known as the coriaceous coelogyne or the rough-leaved coelogyne, is a type of orchid that belongs to the Coelogyne genus. It is a terrestrial orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, with thick, fleshy leaves and a robust stem. The medicinal properties of Coelogyne coriacea include its potential use in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system, with herbal preparations often used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
